_doc: OPTIMAL headline run (not an ablation cell): DINOv3 ViT-H+/16 at the best resolution (688) and the peak LoRA rank (16, alpha 32), all layers adapted. Combines the two ablation optima: rank-16 peak (from the rank sweep) × 688 (still-climbing top of the resolution sweep). Trained to BEST (early-stop on zoom mean localization F1, min 5 epochs, patience 2) — NO epoch-4 cap, because this is a deliverable model, not a comparability cell. 688 trains at batch 8 / grad_accum 1 (effective batch 8). Observed VRAM at batch 2/ga4 was only ~5 GB of 22 (grad checkpointing on + tiny LoRA optimizer state), so the micro-batch is raised all the way to 8 with ga 1 — ONE forward/backward per optimizer step instead of 4, ~4x fewer micro-steps, far better occupancy, ~12 GB used. steps_per_epoch = len(loader)//grad_accum is identical (375) to the batch2/ga4 setting, so the cosine LR schedule is byte-identical and an in-progress run RESUMES cleanly into this (not a restart). To chase the remaining ~10 GB, the next lever is disabling gradient checkpointing (needs a flag) for a faster backward; raising effective batch past 8 would change the LR recipe, so it's avoided. amp fp16 pinned for the L4 (Ada, bf16-capable) to match the fp16 regime of every other run. Colab /content roots + Drive checkpoint_root so it survives runtime disconnect and auto-resumes.
